system.name: Multi-Agent LLM Threat Modeler
system.description: Trust boundary enriched graph. Four trust boundaries identified: Internet API Boundary (if-01), Internet Version Control Boundary (if-02), Internet Supply Chain Boundary (if-03), and Contributor Trust Boundary (if-04).

subsystems.name: Agent Pipeline
subsystems.description: FrameworkOrchestrator and 9 sequential LLM-driven analysis agents sharing a CanonicalGraph state object.

subsystems.name: LLM Integration Layer
subsystems.description: Adapters that translate agent prompts into calls to remote LLMs (XaiAdapter) or local fixture files (FixtureAdapter).

subsystems.name: Supply Chain and Development Environment
subsystems.description: GitHub repository, PyPI package index, developer workstations (Windows 11 or Linux), and CI pipeline used to build, test, and distribute the tool. Includes all contributors, whose accounts represent a potential insider-threat surface.

components.name: FrameworkOrchestrator
components.description: Sequences all 9 agent stages, enforces schema-validation gates at each boundary, and manages HITL checkpoints.

components.name: Agent Registry and BaseAgent
components.description: Maintains the ordered agent map and dispatches run() calls. BaseAgent handles prompt loading and adapter selection.

components.name: Canonical Graph and State
components.description: In-memory Python dataclass holding the evolving threat model graph (subsystems, components, interfaces, STRIDE, threats, mitigations) passed between agents.

components.name: XaiAdapter
components.description: OpenAI-SDK-compatible HTTPS client targeting the xAI Grok inference API. Reads XAI_API_KEY from the environment at call time.

components.name: FixtureAdapter
components.description: Reads pre-generated JSON/text fixture files from disk instead of making live LLM calls. Used for CI and offline testing.

components.name: GitHub Repository
components.description: Remote Git repository hosting source code, agent prompt files, GitHub Actions workflow definitions, and issue tracking.

components.name: Developer Workstation
components.description: Local machine where the tool is authored, tested, and run. May also serve as the primary deployment host for local threat modeling sessions.

components.name: Python venv and pip
components.description: Isolated Python virtual environment and package installer. Resolves and downloads dependencies from PyPI and executes their setup scripts.

functions.name: Pipeline Execution
functions.description: Sequences all 9 agent stages, validates canonical graph at each boundary, and pauses at HITL gates.

functions.name: LLM Completion
functions.description: Serialises the system-prompt and user-message (architecture JSON), sends them to the xAI Grok API over HTTPS, and returns the LLM response text.

functions.name: Source Code Commit and Push
functions.description: Developer commits code locally and pushes to the GitHub repository over SSH or HTTPS.

functions.name: Dependency Installation
functions.description: Resolves and downloads Python packages from PyPI and executes their setup scripts inside the virtual environment.

interfaces.name: XaiAdapter to xAI Grok API
interfaces.description: HTTPS REST calls from XaiAdapter to the remote xAI Grok inference endpoint. Carries system prompts, user messages containing architecture JSON, and LLM responses. XAI_API_KEY is transmitted as a Bearer token.

interfaces.name: Developer Workstation to GitHub
interfaces.description: SSH or HTTPS Git operations (push, pull, clone) between the developer workstation and the GitHub-hosted repository. Carries source code, configuration files, and GitHub Actions workflow definitions.

interfaces.name: pip to PyPI Package Index
interfaces.description: HTTPS requests from pip to PyPI to resolve and download Python packages listed in requirements.txt. Package archives are extracted and setup scripts executed with local user privileges during installation.

interfaces.name: Contributor to GitHub Repository
interfaces.description: Git push operations from individual contributors (including potential insider threats) to the shared repository. Any contributor with write access can modify source code, agent prompt files, or CI workflow definitions.
